_purpose: Example call to amplify_promote_from_memory_md — read a memory-hook log file and surface DRAFT lesson/decision suggestions. Records NOTHING; the operator decides what to keep.
_typical_input_file_format:
### HH:MM — Tool: <name>
### HH:MM — Terminal: `<command>`
### HH:MM — Wrote: <path>

(Any other content in the file is ignored.)
_typical_output_shape:
Memory file: /Users/me/.claude/memory/2026-05-26.md
Total events parsed: 233
Draft suggestions: 5

[1] (repeated_failure, score 1.00) Repeated 75× call: skill_manage
    Suggested type: mistake
    The same call was issued 75 times in this session. Recurring identical calls usually signal a stuck loop or an issue that wasn't actually fixed each iteration.
    Evidence:
      • 00:08 — skill_manage
      • 00:09 — skill_manage
      ...

[2] (decision_candidate, score 0.70) Wrote architectural artifact: aurora-blueprint.md
    Suggested type: decision
    Memory log captured a Wrote event for /Users/me/docs/aurora-blueprint.md at 14:00. Filename suggests an architectural/design artifact that may warrant an amplify_decisions(track) entry.

These are DRAFTS only. Nothing has been recorded yet. Review each one and call amplify_learn or amplify_decisions explicitly for anything worth keeping.

_heuristics_explained.decision_candidate: Wrote: lines pointing at filenames containing plan, decision, architecture, blueprint, design, manifesto, spec, or adr (singular or plural).
_heuristics_explained.intense_session: Any hour with >50 logged events. Dense bursts usually mean something substantive happened that's worth surfacing as an insight.
_heuristics_explained.repeated_failure: The same tool or terminal call issued ≥8 times in a session. Recurring identical calls usually mean a stuck loop or unresolved failure.

_when_to_use: After amplify_audit_freshness flags a stale memory day worth triaging. Or any time you want to retroactively decide what's worth promoting from a long unrecorded session.
